behavior_summary: U8P_EWSHFSID::look is a simple 'U8P_EWSHFSID::look' (examine) process for an in-game object that presents a single short text/bark: "shelf ".
After showing the bark the process suspends/ends, returning control to the engine. No branching, menus, or external interactions are performed.
This function is strictly a display/description handler invoked when the player inspects the object; it does not read or write global flags or call other systems.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: display bark (text output) then suspend/terminate the U8P_EWSHFSID::look process.
No conditional branches, menu presentation, or loops are present.
Contains low-confidence unknown nodes in the raw that bracket the bark, but canonical effect is a single bark + suspend.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the displayed string "shelf " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Do the Unknown nodes correspond to engine framing (open block / suspend) only, or did the original source include additional metadata not reconstructed?
Could there be a longer description accessible via other handlers (use/talk) not present in this fragment?
